methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
RobCo is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Roman Hölzl that has raised $160M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 companies in the robotics and manufacturing sector at the same or adjacent stage, comprising 8 private companies (including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, Dexterity, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $160M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $272M. The peer-median method benchmarks against the 9-company cohort, which carries a median valuation of $1.6B with a range from $300M to $10.0B. This reflects substantial heterogeneity in the peer set, with later-stage and earlier-stage companies spanning an order of magnitude. The peer-median approach directly implies $1.6B.

These two methods triangulate to a range of $231M (low, a 25% haircut to stage-progression) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $936M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects material uncertainty about RobCo's competitive position and execution risk relative to the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include how RobCo's technology and go-to-market compare to later-stage peers like Exotec ($2.0B) and whether current market conditions support peer valuations or warrant recalibration downward.
